name: SAIL Databank
description: The Secure Anonymised Information Linkage (SAIL) Databank is a national data repository for Wales, providing a privacy-protecting platform for linking and analysing person-level data from health, social care, education, housing, and other public services. Uses a unique split-file approach for de-identification, where demographic and clinical data are separated and linked via encrypted matching.
linkages: Cross-sector linkage (health, social care, education, housing)
Welsh Cancer Intelligence and Surveillance Unit
tre_platform: SAIL Gateway (HDR UK-accredited Trusted Research Environment)

requirements.name: IGRP approval
requirements.description: Application reviewed by the Information Governance Review Panel, which includes public representatives and governance experts

requirements.name: SAIL Gateway access
requirements.description: Researchers must access data only within the SAIL Gateway secure environment

requirements.name: Disclosure control
requirements.description: All outputs must pass statistical disclosure control checks before release from the Gateway

access_steps.name: Initial enquiry and feasibility
access_steps.description: Contact SAIL team to discuss research question and data availability

access_steps.name: Submit IGRP application
access_steps.description: Complete and submit the application form to the Information Governance Review Panel

access_steps.name: IGRP review
access_steps.description: Panel reviews application for governance compliance, public benefit, and data minimisation

access_steps.name: Data preparation and provisioning
access_steps.description: SAIL team prepares the approved datasets within the Gateway environment

timeline.notes: Data preparation and Gateway workspace setup

costs.notes: No fees for approved research projects. SAIL is funded by Health and Care Research Wales and Swansea University.

strengths: Rich cross-sector linkage spanning health, social care, education, and housing data
Established and HDR UK-accredited Trusted Research Environment
Unique split-file de-identification approach providing strong privacy protection
Strong track record with 500+ active projects
limitations: Wales only - cannot access data from other UK nations
Smaller population (3.5M) compared to England-based resources
No data extraction - all analysis within SAIL Gateway
IGRP review can take several months
recent_changes: HDR UK accreditation as a Trusted Research Environment
Expanded data holdings including additional social care and housing datasets
Improved Gateway platform with enhanced analytical tools
